title: OpenAI Unveils Enhanced Agents SDK with Native Sandbox Execution
summary: OpenAI has updated its Agents SDK with native sandbox execution and a model-native harness, enabling developers to build secure, long-running agents. This advancement simplifies the integration of AI agents across various files and tools.
body:
OpenAI has released a significant update to its Agents SDK, introducing native sandbox execution and a model-native harness. These features are designed to help developers create secure, long-running agents that can operate across different files and tools. The native sandbox execution ensures that agents run in a controlled environment, reducing the risk of security vulnerabilities.

The new model-native harness allows agents to interact more seamlessly with various tools and APIs, making it easier to build complex workflows. This update is a game-changer for developers who need to deploy AI agents in production environments, as it provides a robust framework for managing agent interactions and ensuring data security.

The future of AI agents looks promising with these enhancements. Developers can now build more sophisticated and secure agents, which could lead to new applications in automation, customer service, and data analysis. The open-source community is likely to embrace these updates, further accelerating the development of AI-driven solutions.
